"Censuses", "Administrative data"], "data_sources_other": "", "open_q1": "To ensure the successful implementation of the NSDSII (2024–2029), Somalia must prioritize the upcoming Housing and Population Census as the foundational dataset for all national planning, while simultaneously modernizing the National Statistical System through digital tools like CAPI and GIS. Strengthening administrative data systems across line ministries and securing a sustainable, government-led funding model are essential to reduce reliance on ad-hoc surveys and ensure the long-term quality of indicators. Furthermore, the system must urgently scale up capacity to address the new \"Climate Change\" pillar (Pillar 6) and enhance coordination between the Federal Government and Member States to provide inclusive, high-quality data that meets the rising policy demands of the National Development Plan.", "open_q2": "", "open_q3": "The Somalia National Strategy for the Development of Statistics (NSDSII) 2024–2029 prioritizes the integration of StatAfric and the Strategy for Harmonization of Statistics in Africa (SHaSA) as a central pillar for regional alignment. To meet these standards, the SNBS requires technical assistance to harmonize indicators across mandated sectors—including integrated economic statistics, governance, and social welfare—while adopting 2025 SNA standards and African trade classifications. Critical capacity needs include advanced methodological training to ensure data comparability, the modernization of statistical business registers National accounts, and the implementation of the Data Quality Assurance Framework (DQAF) to align with the African Charter on Statistics and monitor the Africa Agenda 2063.", "consulted_colleagues": "YES", "submission_id": "1e0a4adb-2312-4249-a31e-9cb4658d1dab", "submitted_at_utc": "2026-02-28T09:00:28Z", "scoring_version": 3}
